ucs.title: [LLM] Naikon (Override Panda) DLL search-order hijack via signed AV/Office binaries loading Aria-body / RainyDay / Nebulae
ucs.description: Hunts Naikon's signature loader pattern: legitimately-signed binaries (Outlook, Kaspersky avp.exe, Avast wsc_proxy, Sandboxie, Symantec, Mcafee mcods.exe) sideloading attacker-supplied DLLs (e.g. aspnet_perf.dll, mscoree.dll, oci.dll, k7sysmon.exe.dll, fltlib.dll) staged in user-writable paths to inject the Aria-body, RainyDay or Nebulae backdoors used against ASEAN government and military targets.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("outlook.exe","avp.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","mcods.exe","SandboxieRpcSs.exe","sandman.exe","k7sysmon.exe","mscorsvw.exe","AdobeUpdate.exe") by host Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.user _time span=1h 
| join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as dll_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("aspnet_perf.dll","mscoree.dll","oci.dll","fltlib.dll","version.dll","DismCore.dll","k7sysmon.exe.dll","Sandboxie.dll","Outllib.dll","libcurl.dll","msvcr100.dll") by host Filesystem.file_path] 
| where like(Processes.process_path, "%\\Users\\%") OR like(Processes.process_path, "%\\ProgramData\\%") OR like(Processes.process_path, "%\\AppData\\%") OR like(Processes.process_path, "%\\Public\\%") OR like(dll_path, "%\\Users\\%") OR like(dll_path, "%\\ProgramData\\%") 
| eval naikon_indicator=if((Processes.process_name="outlook.exe" AND match(dll_name,"Outllib|mscoree")) OR (Processes.process_name="avp.exe" AND match(dll_name,"oci|fltlib")) OR (Processes.process_name="k7sysmon.exe" AND dll_name="k7sysmon.exe.dll"),"high","medium") 
| table firstTime lastTime host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path dll_name dll_path naikon_indicator
ucs.kql:
let naikon_hosts = dynamic(["outlook.exe","avp.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","mcods.exe","SandboxieRpcSs.exe","sandman.exe","k7sysmon.exe","mscorsvw.exe","AdobeUpdate.exe","smtools.exe"]);
let naikon_dlls  = dynamic(["aspnet_perf.dll","mscoree.dll","oci.dll","fltlib.dll","version.dll","DismCore.dll","k7sysmon.exe.dll","Sandboxie.dll","Outllib.dll","libcurl.dll","msvcr100.dll"]);
let writable_paths = dynamic([@"\users\",@"\programdata\",@"\appdata\",@"\public\",@"\temp\",@"\perflogs\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (naikon_hosts)
| where FileName in~ (naikon_dlls)
| extend FolderLower = tolower(FolderPath), HostLower = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| where FolderLower has_any (writable_paths) or HostLower has_any (writable_paths)
| extend signed_host = isnotempty(InitiatingProcessSignerType) and InitiatingProcessSignatureStatus == "Valid"
| where signed_host == true
| extend NaikonHighFidelity = case(
    InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "outlook.exe" and FileName has_any ("Outllib","mscoree"), "AriaBody/Nebulae loader",
    InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "avp.exe" and FileName has_any ("oci","fltlib"), "RainyDay loader",
    InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "k7sysmon.exe" and FileName =~ "k7sysmon.exe.dll", "RainyDay K7 sideload",
    InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "mcods.exe", "Aria-body McAfee sideload",
    "Possible Naikon sideload")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, NaikonHighFidelity
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceProcessEvents | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\Users\\Public","\\ProgramData\\","rundll32","regsvr32") | project DeviceName, ProcessCommandLine, Timestamp) on DeviceName
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: DLL search-order hijacking against AV/Office binaries to load Aria-body (Naikon's flagship RAT documented by Check Point in 2020), RainyDay and Nebulae is the defining tradecraft of Naikon / G0019 / Override Panda. The specific pairings (avp.exe+oci.dll, k7sysmon.exe+k7sysmon.exe.dll, outlook.exe+Outllib.dll, mcods.exe sideload) are unique fingerprints documented in Check Point, Bitdefender and Ka

ucs.title: [LLM] Naikon RoyalRoad RTF lure → schtasks persistence → internal recon chain (G0019 spearphishing playbook)
ucs.description: Catches Naikon's end-to-end intrusion chain against Southeast-Asian diplomatic and military targets: weaponised RTF (RoyalRoad/8.t builder shared with TA428/Goblin Panda) delivered by spearphish, dropping a payload that registers a Scheduled Task and immediately performs SMB/AD reconnaissance (net view, nbtstat, T1018/T1046) — the same sequence observed in Naikon's 'Aria-body' campaigns against ASEAN ministries.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Email where Email.file_name IN ("*.rtf","*.doc","*.docx") by Email.recipient Email.src_user Email.subject Email.message_id _time span=1h 
| join type=inner Email.recipient [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Processes.process) as proc_cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as proc_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","eqnedt32.exe") (Processes.process_name IN ("schtasks.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*\\8.t*","*Intel*.exe*","*RasTls*")) by host Processes.user _rename Email.recipient=Processes.user] 
| append [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe","nbtstat.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe") (Processes.process IN ("*view /domain*","*group \"domain admins\"*","*-A *","* /all*")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name _time span=10m] 
| stats values(Email.subject) as subject values(proc_name) as proc_chain values(Processes.process) as recon_cmds count by host 
| where count >= 3 
| eval naikon_score = if(match(mvjoin(proc_chain,","),"eqnedt32|schtasks") AND mvcount(recon_cmds)>=2,"high","medium")
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 24h;
let rtf_recipients =
    EmailAttachmentInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where FileName has_any (".rtf",".doc",".docx")
    | join kind=inner (EmailEvents | where Timestamp > ago(lookback) and DeliveryAction == "Delivered") on NetworkMessageId
    | project Timestamp, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, Subject, FileName, SHA256, NetworkMessageId;
let rr_execution =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","eqnedt32.exe","excel.exe")
    | where FileName in~ ("schtasks.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wmic.exe")
        or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("8.t","intel.wll","RasTls","\\Intel\\","\\Public\\","aria-body","AppData\\Roaming\\Intel")
    | project ExecTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountUpn=InitiatingProcessAccountUpn, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine;
let recon =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe","nbtstat.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","ipconfig.exe","whoami.exe","ping.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("view /domain","group \"domain admins\"","/all","-A ","-an","/domain")
    | summarize ReconCount=dcount(FileName), ReconCmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,10), FirstRecon=min(Timestamp), LastRecon=max(Timestamp) by DeviceName, AccountUpn=InitiatingProcessAccountUpn;
rr_execution
| join kind=inner rtf_recipients on $left.AccountUpn == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| where ExecTime between (Timestamp .. Timestamp + 2h)
| join kind=inner recon on DeviceName, AccountUpn
| where FirstRecon between (ExecTime .. ExecTime + 1h) and ReconCount >= 2
| extend NaikonChain="Phish RTF -> Office child -> schtasks/persist -> AD recon"
| project Timestamp, SenderFromAddress, Subject, FileName, SHA256, DeviceName, AccountUpn, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine, ReconCmds, NaikonChain
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Naikon's documented playbook (CameraShy report, Check Point 'Naikon's New Backdoor', Bitdefender 2020) consistently chains RoyalRoad-style RTF lures (CVE-2017-11882 via eqnedt32.exe, or .wll Word add-in T1137.006) into immediate scheduled-task persistence and aggressive 'net view /domain' / nbtstat AD enumeration to map ASEAN ministry networks before deploying Aria-body. Joining the email recipien
